checks.a_analysis: The output document faithfully represents the text from the source PDF. All headings ('NC Parcels', 'NC Parcels Project Status by County', 'Thank you!') and body text (dates, sources, contact labels) correspond directly to content found in the original document. There are no fabricated paragraphs, sentences, or section headings.

The contact information on page 3 of the source PDF consists of three email addresses: 'katie.doherty@rutherfordcountync.gov', 'relkins@sosnc.gov', and 'elizabeth.daniel@nc.gov'. All three are accurately captured in the output JSON with correct mailto links and text labels. No fabricated contact information was introduced.

The output JSON contains three sections, corresponding to the three pages of the source PDF. Each page's primary text elements and images are represented in the structured output. No sections or pages were omitted.
The source document contains no tables; it consists of text, logos, and an infographic map. Therefore, this check is not applicable.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: All text found in the source document is present in the output JSON. The map legend and labels on page 2 are part of the graphical element and are appropriately summarized in the image's alt text. The source list on page 2 and the contact list on page 3 are fully preserved.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: A thorough comparison between the source PDF and the JSON output confirms that all text, including critical dates ('April 10, 2025' and 'April 9, 2025') and proper nouns, has been transcribed correctly. There are no minor text differences that alter the meaning or information provided by the document.

checks.a_analysis: The output contains the NC DIT logo on each page (page 1, 2, and 3). However, this logo appears in the same relative position on every page of the source PDF. This constitutes intentional repetition (standard header/footer element) and is not a defect according to the audit guidelines. No other text or structural elements are duplicated.

The output uses Heading 2 (H2) for all major titles on each page ('NC Parcels', 'NC Parcels Project Status by County', and 'Thank you!'). While there is no Heading 1 (H1), the hierarchy is flat and consistent across sections without any skips (e.g., no H2 skipping to H4). Heading level adjustments for accessibility are permitted under the guidelines.
The source document does not contain any tables. The audit for table structure is not applicable.

The reading order in the output follows the logical flow of the source document. Page 1 starts with the title, then date, then logo. Page 2 follows with the title, date, map, and then footer elements (logos and sources). Page 3 ends with 'Thank you!', contact details, and the final logo. This is a consistent and logical top-to-bottom reading order.

The 'Sources' section on page 2 consists of separate lines of text in the PDF. The conversion has rendered these as individual paragraphs. While they could be seen as a list, the semantic meaning and structural separation are preserved. No numbered or bulleted lists in the source were missed or corrupted.
The output correctly identifies and converts the three email addresses on page 3 into clickable 'mailto:' links. The text and destination URLs match the source exactly.

checks.a_analysis: The converted HTML maintains the overall visual organization and flow of the original document. It accurately captures the sequence of the presentation: title information, the project status map, and the concluding contact page. The transition to a single-column HTML layout with a navigation sidebar is an expected structural change for accessibility and responsiveness, and it does not disrupt the visual logic of the content.
The source document does not contain any tables; therefore, no table rendering issues are present.

All meaningful images and logos from the source PDF are present and correctly rendered in the HTML output. This includes the branding logos (NC DIT, NCGICC) and the central map graphic. No broken image icons or missing meaningful graphics were observed.

There is clear visual duplication of content from page 2 of the PDF. The title 'NC Parcels Project Status by County', the date 'April 9, 2025', and the 'Sources' text block are rendered as HTML text. However, these same elements are also fully visible within the map image immediately adjacent to them. This creates a repetitive visual experience where the same information block is displayed twice in succession.
checks.b_evidence_source: Source PDF Page 2 contains a title 'NC Parcels Project Status by County', date 'April 9, 2025', and a 'Sources' section as part of the page layout.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot displays the title and date as text, followed by the map image which contains the same title and date, followed again by the 'Sources' text block which is also contained within the image.
checks.b_evidence_location: HTML section titled 'NC Parcels Project Status by County'
